This was a tough one, some things good and some things not so good. That being said these folks just opened a week ago so some grace is in order. At entering LTA there was a pronounced unpleasant odor throughout. I could not identify it, not grease or even food. It may be an odor from the new furniture, but it set up an unpleasant experience. The staff was friendly and inviting and we enjoyed the strong effort staff made to accommodate us. The food taste left much to be desired with the exception of the rice. The rice was some of the best I have ever tasted, fluffy and flavor-able.
I suppose it should be said we waited months for them to open and it is possible we allowed our expectations not to be practical. I do want to commend the staff who were watching us and our body language and noticed we were concerned about the flavor of the food. They offered to replace any items and I believe would not have charged us anything had we complained, but we do not do that in public.
We will be going back for another visit to try some different menu items and to see if the odor was a temporary issue, but based on 30.00 for the two of us with no drinks we don't have much in the way of expectations for the future. If I find different I will renew this review.
Again, these guys seem to be making an effort so we have hope it will improve.

review_text:
Went to check out the new Mexican restaurant on the block, as we usually go to Old Mexico just east on 5 mile. Took my wife and two teen girls that just love Mexican food. The girls ordered a nacho platter, my wife ordered a chicken burrito, and I decided to try the Beef tacos.
We ordered Margaritas that were basically weak lime juice and a scoop of ice, so we sent them back for bottled beer. Easy to get that right. When our food came after a long wait, it was cold, the nacho platter had cold, grated cheese on it, and a mound of shreeded lettuce on top that you had to dig through to get to the chips, like some demented, corn chip salad. Most disheartening.
My wife was had the most quizzical look on her face after every other bite, as if she couldnt believe what she was tasting, so I tried a bite. The flavor could only be described as vaguely chicken, with an overpowering cilantro flavor that she kept drowning in hot sauce to make palatable.
As stated before, I recieved room temperature beef tacos, with grease saturated shells, with cheese and warm, flaccid lettuce, similar to the consistency of microwaved taco bell, which certainly has them beat in the flavor and price department. The girls joked that we would've been better served there, especially since it is just next door. If you are contemplating going here, I would recommend grab some Taco bell, and sit in Los tres Amigos for the ambience. Its the only thing they have going for them.

Lousy service -
I joined my friend who arrived minutes before me, shortly after 6 pm on Friday November 3rd. The restaurant was busy, but there was no wait to be seated. The minute I arrived our waiter wanted to take our order, before I had a chance to look at the menu. He left and never returned. We were left with chips and salsa. While he was gone, we ordered the guacamole made table side from another employee. We finally had to order margaritas from a waitress at an adjoining section. My friend ordered a house Margarita at happy hour price. I made the mistake of ordering a Specialty one from the menu which had no prices listed. Although I asked for the price, the waitress placed the order and our waiter brought our drinks and took our order - finally. By this time, we had waited nearly 45 minutes for our waiter to return. I'm a person that can't handle spicy food. That being said, the salsa here had me reaching for the hot sauce because it was so bland. It was nearly tomato paste. A few jalenpenos slivers and some pico de gallo would be an easy improvement. The rest of the food was of similar quality. My chicken quesadilla was tasty, but it was smaller than expected, not cut, and the corners weren't pressed so it fell apart very quickly. The pico that came with it was an actual slice of tomato like you'd put on a sandwich, which was strange.

My drink was surprisingly delicious, despite the fact that I was told they didn't have a bartender working that night and had to look up how to make it. (it was on the menu). Our waitress was very nice and attentive. The decor is really cool and the bathroom was clean and stocked. I would go back, but only if they improve the food with some training and spices in the kitchen.

review_text:
Normal date night.. I picked this restaurant because I wanted Mexican food. Service and servicer were fine. Ordering was easy and servicer was able to answer questions about menu items with ease. My dinner arrived. To my dismay my burrito was half filled and the plate was disheveled. There was dripping sour cream and wilting greens on top. The portion of beans was less than 3 oz, smeared across the plate and a half cup of reheated rice.

My boyfriend ordered four enchiladas. The beans ran from one as did the cheese from another. His meat was unseasoned and he did not receive his rice. His lemonade was flavorless.

I give my experience at Los Tres Amigos one star for the server. She was pretty good. However from the salsa to the meal, everything tasted salty or prepackaged. I am now home praying that I do not get sick.

I will not recommend dining here.

After the waiter took the drink away, our waiters seem to switch two or three times. Meaning that we weren’t sure what happened to our waiter because we seen a few different people servicing our table.  The second waiter that we had said that he was taking over our table.

When we got our bill, it was only the credit card receipt and not a detailed receipt. Although I calculated it in my head, I couldn’t find the waiter to ask for the detailed copy. So we just signed and left.

About an hour after getting home both my girlfriend and I were nauseated and felt very sick with stomach pain.  I am not the type to write a review, but still as it is almost 24 hours later, we are both still feeling nauseated.

Needless to say, we will not be returning here.
